title: PhosphoLIMBO: An Easy and Efficient Protocol to Separate and Analyze Phospholipids by HPTLC From Plant Material
author: Louise Fougère
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Primulin solution (see Recipes) Recipes Note: For safety reasons, we recommend using solvents under an extractor hood and wearing personal protective equipment: gloves, lab coat, and safety goggles. When using acids such as HCl, always add the acid to the water with the same safety measures as for solvents.
Murashige and Skoog powder (Duchefa Biochemie, catalog number: M0222.

steps.title: MilliQ water (H 2 O) (sterile and non sterile)
steps.instruction: MilliQ water (H 2 O) (sterile and non sterile)

steps.title: HEPES solution Reagent Final concentration Amount...
steps.instruction: HEPES solution Reagent Final concentration Amount HEPES 50 mM 12 g H 2 O n/a 1,000 mL Adjust pH to 7.5 with KOH. Note: Can be stored at 4 °C for a few months.

steps.title: 2 M MgCl 2 Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 2 M MgCl 2 Reagent Final concentration Amount MgCl 2 2 M 9.52 g H 2 O n/a 50 mL Note: The MgCl 2 powder must be added very cautiously inside glass containers (due to exothermic reaction in water).

steps.title: 1 M Dithiothreitol Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 1 M Dithiothreitol Reagent Final concentration Amount Dithiothreitol 1 M 7.712 g H 2 O n/a 50 mL Note: Can be stored at -20 °C for a few months.

steps.title: Membrane extraction buffer Reagent Final concentration Amount Sucrose 0
steps.instruction: Membrane extraction buffer Reagent Final concentration Amount Sucrose 0.45 M 77 g MgCl 2 5 mM 1.25 mL (from 2 M stock solution) Dithiothreitol 1 mM 500 μL (from 1 M stock solution) Polyvinylpyrrolidone 0.5 g/100 mL 2.5 g HEPES (50 mM, pH 7.

steps.title: n/a 500 mL Note
steps.instruction: n/a 500 mL Note: Can be stored at -20 °C for a few months.

steps.title: PMSF solution Reagent Final concentration Amount PMSF 100 mM 0
steps.instruction: PMSF solution Reagent Final concentration Amount PMSF 100 mM 0.174 g DMSO n/a 10 mL PMSF 1 mM needs to be prepared freshly in the membrane extraction buffer. Note: Can be stored at -20 °C for a few months.

steps.title: 38% sucrose solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 38% sucrose solution Reagent Final concentration Amount Sucrose 38 g/100 mL 38 g HEPES (50 mM, pH 7.

steps.title: n/a 100 mL Note
steps.instruction: n/a 100 mL Note: Can be stored at -20 °C for a few months.

steps.title: Membrane resuspension buffer Reagent Final concentration Amount Sucrose 0
steps.instruction: Membrane resuspension buffer Reagent Final concentration Amount Sucrose 0.25 M 0.34 g MgCl 2 (2 M) 1.5 mM 3 μL EDTA (0.5 M, pH
0.2 mM 1.6 μL NaCl (5 M) 150 mM 120 μL HEPES (50 mM, pH 7.

steps.title: n/a 4 mL PMSF 1 mM...
steps.instruction: n/a 4 mL PMSF 1 mM 40 μL Cocktail Protease Inhibitor 1 mL/100 mL 40 μL Note: PMSF and cocktail protease inhibitor must be added at the last moment and kept on ice.
0.25% KCl Reagent % solution Amount KCl 0.25% 0.25 g Water n/a 100 mL

steps.title: 2 M HCl Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 2 M HCl Reagent Final concentration Amount HCl (37% fumant) 2 M 9.86 mL Water n/a 30.14 mL

steps.title: 1 M HCl Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 1 M HCl Reagent Final concentration Amount HCl (2 M) 1 M 25 mL Water n/a 50 mL
0.01 M HCl Reagent Final concentration Amount HCl (1 M) 0.01 M 500 μL Water n/a 50 mL

steps.title: Phospholipid extraction “Kill” mix Reagent % solution Amount Methanol 64
steps.instruction: Phospholipid extraction “Kill” mix Reagent % solution Amount Methanol 64.57% 484 mL Chloroform 32.29% 242 mL HCl (1 M) 3.14% 24 mL Total n/a 750 mL Shake vigorously by hand.

steps.title: Phospholipid wash mix Reagent % solution Amount Methanol 26
steps.instruction: Phospholipid wash mix Reagent % solution Amount Methanol 26.66% 12 mL Chloroform 53.34% 24 mL HCl (0.01 M) 20% 9 mL Total n/a 45 mL Shake vigorously by hand. Let the solution stabilize for a few minutes in a two-phase solution.

steps.title: 10× PBS Reagent Final concentration Amount...
steps.instruction: 10× PBS Reagent Final concentration Amount Na 2 HPO 4 ·2H 2 O 2.05 g/L 2.05 g KH 2 PO 4 240 mg/L 0.24 g NaCl 800 mg/L 0.8 g KCl 200 mg/L 0.2 g Water n/a 1,000 mL Adjust pH at 7.

steps.title: Shake vigorously by hand. Note
steps.instruction: Shake vigorously by hand. Note: Can be stored at room temperature on the bench.

steps.title: Migration solvent mix, PhosphoLIMBO Reagent Final concentration Amount Chloroform 30
steps.instruction: Migration solvent mix, PhosphoLIMBO Reagent Final concentration Amount Chloroform 30.3% 5 mL Methanol 10.4% 1.7 mL 2-propanol 24.2%

steps.title: mL KCl (0
steps.instruction: mL KCl (0.25%) 7.9% 1.3 mL Methyl acetate 24.2% 4 mL Triethylamine 3% 0.5 mL Note: We recommend preparing it freshly.

steps.title: Primulin solution Reagent Final concentration Amount Primulin 0
steps.instruction: Primulin solution Reagent Final concentration Amount Primulin 0.1 g/L 20 mg Acetone 50% 100 mL PBS (10×) 1× 20 mL Water n/a 80 mL Shake vigorously by hand and let it sit for some minutes on the bench. Note: We recommend preparing it freshly. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Proceed with stratification of Col-0 seeds...
steps.instruction: Proceed with stratification of Col-0 seeds by addition of 1 mL of water in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and incubation at 4 °C for 3 days with approximately 200 µL of seeds (corresponding to 120–160 mg of dry seeds).

steps.title: Carry out sterilization of the seeds...
steps.instruction: Carry out sterilization of the seeds with a bleach solution for 25 min, followed by three 3 min washes with sterile MilliQ water.

steps.title: Prepare a culture flask with 250...
steps.instruction: Prepare a culture flask with 250 mL of half Murashige and Skoog ( 1 / 2 MS) liquid media and sterilize it at 110 °C for 30 min.

steps.title: In sterile conditions and when the...
steps.instruction: In sterile conditions and when the medium is back to room temperature, transfer the seeds to the flask under sterile conditions.

steps.title: Place the flask on a table...
steps.instruction: Place the flask on a table shaker at 110 rpm in a culture condition room (16 h light/8 h darkness at 22 °C) for 7 days.

steps.title: Cool down a mortar, a pestle,...
steps.instruction: Cool down a mortar, a pestle, and 50 mL Falcon tubes of membrane extraction buffer on ice.

steps.title: Collect the plants, remove the excess...
steps.instruction: Collect the plants, remove the excess liquid culture, and weigh their mass to harvest a minimum of 10 g of humid plant weight.

steps.title: Rapidly deposit them in the mortar...
steps.instruction: Rapidly deposit them in the mortar and grind them with a pestle by adding 2–3 volumes (v/w) of membrane extraction buffer + PMSF 1 mM (final concentration) prepared freshly.

steps.title: When the plant solution is correctly...
steps.instruction: When the plant solution is correctly crushed (when a homogeneous green solution is obtained), filter it with a gauze installed inside a funnel above a 50 mL Falcon.

steps.title: Centrifuge the Falcon three times at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the Falcon three times at 936 rcf for 10 min at 4 °C. The purpose of this step is to remove cell debris. Thus, at the end of each centrifugation, transfer the supernatant to a new Falcon and trash the Falcon containing the pellet.

steps.title: In ultracentrifugation tubes of 12 mL,...
steps.instruction: In ultracentrifugation tubes of 12 mL, add to the bottom 2 mL of 38% sucrose solution. Very carefully, add around 9–10 mL of the plant homogenate above this cushion of sucrose, without disturbing the interface.

steps.title: Equilibrate the ultracentrifugation rotor and start...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the ultracentrifugation rotor and start the ultracentrifugation at 122,253× g for 2 h at 4 °C.

steps.title: Using a glass Pasteur pipette, harvest...
steps.instruction: Using a glass Pasteur pipette, harvest the yellow membrane phase present at the interface between the cushion of sucrose and the supernatant and place it in a 15 mL Falcon tube. Note: If the volume of membrane harvested is more than 9 mL, split the volume collected into two Falcons of 50 mL

steps.title: Fill the 15 mL Falcon to...
steps.instruction: Fill the 15 mL Falcon to 12 mL with HEPES 50 mM solution and mix by inversion.

steps.title: Transfer to new ultracentrifugation tubes of 12 mL
steps.instruction: Transfer to new ultracentrifugation tubes of 12 mL.

steps.title: Equilibrate the ultracentrifugation rotor and start...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the ultracentrifugation rotor and start the ultracentrifugation at 122,253× g for 2 h at 4 °C.

steps.title: At the end of the ultracentrifugation,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the ultracentrifugation, discard the supernatant carefully and resuspend the pellet with 0.2–1 mL of membrane resuspension buffer (depending on the size of the pellet). The microsomal fraction (total membrane fraction) is obtained and can be stored for a short time at 4 °C or at -80 °C for longer-term storage. Note: If there is more than one pellet for one condition, pool them. Note: We recommend performing protein quantification with a Bicinchoninic Acid (BCA) Protein Assay kit at this step to estimate the amount of material in this microsomal fraction before directly performing phospholipid extraction by the addition of 725 µL of Phospholipid extraction “Kill” mix solution to the pellet (see step B1). We recommend obtaining a minimum of 2 mg/mL of proteins before proceeding to the phospholipid extraction. B. Extraction of phospholipids of the previously obtained microsomal fraction The phospholipid extraction protocol described was adapted from [11], which was established from biological samples.

steps.title: Add 725 μL of phospholipid extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 725 μL of phospholipid extraction “Kill” mix and 150 μL of MilliQ water in the total membrane fraction sample and transfer it to a 2 mL Eppendorf.

steps.title: Add 750 μL of chloroform
steps.instruction: Add 750 μL of chloroform.

steps.title: Add 170 μL of 2 M HCl
steps.instruction: Add 170 μL of 2 M HCl.

steps.title: Shake vigorously by hand
steps.instruction: Shake vigorously by hand.

steps.title: Vortex for 15 s
steps.instruction: Vortex for 15 s.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,500× g for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,500× g for 5 min

steps.title: Remove the upper phase and transfer...
steps.instruction: Remove the upper phase and transfer the lower phase to a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Note: Be careful at this step to limit the interactions and exchanges between the upper and lower phases

steps.title: Take 710 μL of the upper...
steps.instruction: Take 710 μL of the upper phase of the phospholipid wash mix and add it to the 2 mL Eppendorf tube that contains the lower phase of step B

steps.title: Shake vigorously by hand
steps.instruction: Shake vigorously by hand.

steps.title: Vortex for several seconds
steps.instruction: Vortex for several seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,500× g for 3 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,500× g for 3 min.

steps.title: Remove the upper phase and transfer...
steps.instruction: Remove the upper phase and transfer the lower phase to a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Repeat step B8 two more times. Note
steps.instruction: Repeat step B8 two more times. Note: Be careful to delete the water drops in the upper phase at each step.

steps.title: This lipid extract can be stored...
steps.instruction: This lipid extract can be stored at -20 °C in a 2 mL HPLC vial. C. Preparation of the migration solvent (under the fume hood)

steps.title: Prepare the PhosphoLIMBO mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the PhosphoLIMBO mix. a. Add 5 mL of chloroform to a 25 mL glassware. b. Add 1.7 mL of methanol. c. Add 4.2 mL of 2-propanol. d. Add 1.3 mL of KCl at 0.25%. e. Add 4 mL of methyl acetate. f. Add 0.5 mL of triethylamine.

steps.title: Mix the migration solvent by agitation
steps.instruction: Mix the migration solvent by agitation.

steps.title: Wash the CAMAG twin trough chamber with methanol
steps.instruction: Wash the CAMAG twin trough chamber with methanol.

steps.title: Wash the CAMAG twin trough chamber...
steps.instruction: Wash the CAMAG twin trough chamber a second time with some migration solvent (approximately 2 or 3 mL).

steps.title: Deposit the entire volume of the...
steps.instruction: Deposit the entire volume of the migration solvent in the CAMAG twin trough chamber.

steps.title: Close it immediately after
steps.instruction: Close it immediately after.

steps.title: Split the migration solvent equitably between...
steps.instruction: Split the migration solvent equitably between the two gutters of the TLC developing chamber.

steps.title: Wait for 25/30 min for the chamber to be saturated
steps.instruction: Wait for 25/30 min for the chamber to be saturated. Note: To limit evaporation, we advise adding a heavy object (like a glass bottle) to the CAMAG twin trough chamber during the entire saturation period and the migration step. D. Loading of the lipid extract on an HPTLC plate As a control of migration, phospholipid standards are loaded on the plate, alone or in a mix. Please refer to General notes 3, 4, and

steps.title: Turn on the Linomat system to...
steps.instruction: Turn on the Linomat system to proceed to the loading of the plate.

steps.title: Prepare the washing solution (chloroform/methanol 2:1 v/v)
steps.instruction: Prepare the washing solution (chloroform/methanol 2:1 v/v).

steps.title: Wash the syringe 3 times with the washing solution
steps.instruction: Wash the syringe 3 times with the washing solution.

steps.title: Prepare the loading plan on the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the loading plan on the HPTLC plate using the linomat software VisionCATs. a. Plate format: 20 cm × 10 cm b. Width: 200 mm c. Height: 100 mm d. Deposit height: 8.0 mm e. First track position ≥ 30.0 mm Note: Be careful with side effects during migration; do not load lipid extract too close to the edge of the plate.

steps.title: Place the HPTLC Silica gel 60...
steps.instruction: Place the HPTLC Silica gel 60 F254 20 cm × 10 cm on the linomat stage.

steps.title: Run the deposit procedure for the samples. Note
steps.instruction: Run the deposit procedure for the samples. Note: Do not forget to wash the syringe 3 times with the full volume of the syringe, e.g., 100 μL, between each sample using the washing solution.

steps.title: Let the plate dry on a...
steps.instruction: Let the plate dry on a vertical position for 5 min at room temperature. E. Migration procedure

steps.title: Place the HPTLC plate vertically in...
steps.instruction: Place the HPTLC plate vertically in one of the two hinges of the CAMAG twin trough chamber and close it rapidly to limit the loss of the saturation solvent in the chamber. Note: The plate must be slightly immersed in the migration solvent and as close as possible to the inside face of the migration chamber.

steps.title: Wait without disturbing the migration (do...
steps.instruction: Wait without disturbing the migration (do not move the tank during the migration).

steps.title: Wait until the migration solvent has...
steps.instruction: Wait until the migration solvent has reached the limit of the upper edge of the plate (until 2–3 mm from the upper edge). Note: For a 10-cm high plate, wait approximately 50–55 min.

steps.title: At the end of the migration,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the migration, take out the plate of the tank and let it dry vertically under the fume hood. F. Visualization of lipid separation on the HPTLC plate The revelation of lipids is performed using primulin, which is a non-destructive lipid dye interacting in a nonspecific manner with the acyl chain of lipids (refer to General note 6).

steps.title: Transfer the primulin solution into a glass recipient
steps.instruction: Transfer the primulin solution into a glass recipient.

steps.title: Carefully submerge the plate and take it out immediately
steps.instruction: Carefully submerge the plate and take it out immediately. Note: Do so in such a way that the primulin solution covers the plate evenly.

steps.title: Let the plate dry for 10...
steps.instruction: Let the plate dry for 10 min vertically in the dark.

steps.title: Reveal the plate by UV light...
steps.instruction: Reveal the plate by UV light (302 nm) using the ChemiDoc system and the Image Lab TM software.

description: Validation of protocol The PhosphoLIMBO protocol was used in previously published studies [3,19]. The figures of this article further validate the protocol by quantifying the Rf value, confirming the separation of all phospholipid classes by statistical tests, and testing its efficiency in biological samples.
